{{日本の寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |画像=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |所在地=大阪府池田市鉢塚3-4-6 |位置={{coor d|34.818009|N|135.439094|E}} |山号 =鉢多羅山 |宗旨 = |宗派 =[[高野山真言宗]] |本尊 =[[釈迦如来]] |創建年 = [[神亀]]元年（724年） |開基 =[[行基菩薩]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 =鉢多羅山 釈迦院 |別称 = |札所等 =[[摂津八十八ヶ所霊場]] 第五十七番霊場 |文化財 =宝篋印塔 |公式HP = http://www7.ocn.ne.jp/~shakain/index.html |公式HP名 = 尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }}
'''釈迦院'''（しゃかいん）は[[大阪府]][[池田市]]鉢塚にある[[寺院]]。
==歴史== 寺伝によれば、[[神功皇后]]が[[百済]]より[[釈尊]]の[[仏舎利]]多羅宝鉢を得て凱旋し、[[仏教]]がまだ広まっていないので、[[仲哀天皇]]の御代、散逸を恐れて鉢塚に隠した。 その後、[[行基]]が霊夢により石窟より仏舎利を掘出し、[[聖武天皇]]の[[勅命]]で精舎を建立。また、行基は自ら[[観音菩薩]]・[[不動明王]]・[[毘沙門天]]像を刻して仏舎利とともに安置。寺を鉢多羅山若王寺釈迦院と号し、村を尊鉢と名づけ、斎田300石を賜った。 [[天正]]年間（1573年～1592年）の兵火で[[堂宇]]と寺宝を焼失し、斎田も没収されたが、天正17年（1589年）に傳誉が再建し、その後[[天保]]11年（1840年）火災に遭い、[[慶応]]元年（1865年）6月宗浄・歓浄が再建<ref>大阪府学務部「大阪府史跡名勝天然記念物」（1974）</ref>。
==文化財== [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|宝篋印塔]] * [[宝篋印塔]] [[国指定重要文化財]]（昭和10年10月14日指定）[[鎌倉時代]] [[花崗岩]]製、「[[正安]]元年（1299年）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正」と刻銘がある。 * [[梵鐘]] 池田市[[指定文化財]] [[江戸時代]] [[寛永]]九壬申年（1632年）の銘があり、[[池田市]]内最古のものの一つ。
==出典== <references/>
==関連項目== *[[鉢塚古墳 (池田市)]] *[[五社神社 (池田市)]]
{{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しゃかいん}} [[Category:大阪府の寺]] [[Category:池田市]]

'''釈迦院'''（しゃかいん）は[[大阪府]][[池田市]]鉢塚にある[[寺院]]。
==歴史== 寺伝によれば、[[神功皇后]]が[[百済]]より[[釈尊]]の[[仏舎利]]多羅宝鉢を得て凱旋し、[[仏教]]がまだ広まっていないので、[[仲哀天皇]]の御代、散逸を恐れて鉢塚に隠した。 その後、[[行基]]が霊夢により石窟より仏舎利を掘出し、[[聖武天皇]]の[[勅命]]で精舎を建立。また、行基は自ら[[観音菩薩]]・[[不動明王]]・[[毘沙門天]]像を刻して仏舎利とともに安置。寺を鉢多羅山若王寺釈迦院と号し、村を尊鉢と名づけ、斎田300石を賜った。 [[天正]]年間（1573年～1592年）の兵火で[[堂宇]]と寺宝を焼失し、斎田も没収されたが、天正17年（1589年）に傳誉が再建し、その後[[天保]]11年（1840年）火災に遭い、[[慶応]]元年（1865年）6月宗浄・歓浄が再建<ref>大阪府学務部「大阪府史跡名勝天然記念物」（1974）</ref>。
==文化財== [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|宝篋印塔]] * [[宝篋印塔]] [[国指定重要文化財]]（昭和10年10月14日指定）[[鎌倉時代]] [[花崗岩]]製、「[[正安]]元年（1299年）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正」と刻銘がある。 * [[梵鐘]] 池田市[[指定文化財]] [[江戸時代]] [[寛永]]九壬申年（1632年）の銘があり、[[池田市]]内最古のものの一つ。

==関連項目== *[[鉢塚古墳 (池田市)]] *[[五社神社 (池田市)]]

'''釈迦院'''（しゃかいん）は[[大阪府]][[池田市]]鉢塚にある[[寺院]]。
== 歴史 == 寺伝によれば、[[神功皇后]]が[[百済]]より[[釈尊]]の[[仏舎利]]多羅宝鉢を得て凱旋し、[[仏教]]がまだ広まっていないので、[[仲哀天皇]]の御代、散逸を恐れて鉢塚に隠した。 その後、[[行基]]が霊夢により石窟より仏舎利を掘出し、[[聖武天皇]]の[[勅命]]で精舎を建立。また、行基は自ら[[観音菩薩]]・[[不動明王]]・[[毘沙門天]]像を刻して仏舎利とともに安置。寺を鉢多羅山若王寺釈迦院と号し、村を尊鉢と名づけ、斎田300石を賜った。 [[天正]]年間（1573年～1592年）の兵火で[[堂宇]]と寺宝を焼失し、斎田も没収されたが、天正17年（1589年）に傳誉が再建し、その後[[天保]]11年（1840年）火災に遭い、[[慶応]]元年（1865年）6月宗浄・歓浄が再建<ref>大阪府学務部「大阪府史跡名勝天然記念物」（1974）</ref>。
== 文化財 == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|宝篋印塔]] * [[宝篋印塔]] [[鎌倉時代]] :[[花崗岩]]製、「[[正安]]元年（1299年）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正」と刻銘がある。 * [[梵鐘]] 池田市[[指定文化財]] [[江戸時代]] :[[寛永]]九壬申年（1632年）の銘があり、[[池田市]]内最古のものの一つ。

== 歴史 == 寺伝によれば、[[神功皇后]]が[[百済]]より[[釈尊]]の[[仏舎利]]多羅宝鉢を得て凱旋し、[[仏教]]がまだ広まっていないので、[[仲哀天皇]]の御代、散逸を恐れて鉢塚に隠した。 その後、[[行基]]が霊夢により石窟より仏舎利を掘出し、[[聖武天皇]]の[[勅命]]で精舎を建立。また、行基は自ら[[観音菩薩]]・[[不動明王]]・[[毘沙門天]]像を刻して仏舎利とともに安置。寺を鉢多羅山若王寺釈迦院と号し、村を尊鉢と名づけ、斎田300石を賜った。 [[天正]]年間（1573年～1592年）の兵火で[[堂宇]]と寺宝を焼失し、斎田も没収されたが、天正17年（1589年）に傳誉が再建し、その後[[天保]]11年（1840年）火災に遭い、[[慶応]]元年（1865年）6月宗浄・歓浄が再建<ref>大阪府学務部「大阪府史跡名勝天然記念物」（1974）</ref>。

{{日本の寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |画像=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |所在地=大阪府池田市鉢塚3丁目4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号 =[[鉢多羅山]] |院号 =若王寺[[釋迦院]] |宗旨 = |宗派 =[[高野山真言宗]] |本尊 =[[釈迦如来]] |創建年 = [[神亀]]元年（724年） |開基 =[[行基菩薩]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 鉢多羅山 釈迦院 |別称 = 鉢多羅山若王寺釋迦院
[[尊鉢厄神]] |札所等 =[[摂津八十八ヶ所霊場]] 第57番霊場 |文化財 = |公式HP = http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名 = 尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''釈迦院'''（しゃかいん）は[[大阪府]][[池田市]][[鉢塚]]にある[[高野山真言宗]]の仏教[[寺院]]。 == 歴史 == 寺伝によれば、[[神功皇后]]が[[百済]]より[[釈尊]]の[[仏舎利]]多羅宝鉢を得て凱旋し、[[仏教]]がまだ広まっていないので、[[仲哀天皇]]の御代、散逸を恐れて鉢塚に隠した。 その後、[[行基]]が霊夢により石窟より仏舎利を掘出し、[[聖武天皇]]の[[勅命]]で精舎を建立。また、行基は自ら[[観音菩薩]]・[[不動明王]]・[[毘沙門天]]像を刻して仏舎利とともに安置。寺を鉢多羅山若王寺釈迦院と号し、村を尊鉢と名づけ、[[斎田]]300石を賜った。 [[天正]]年間（1573年～1592年）の兵火で[[若王寺]]は[[堂宇]]と寺宝を焼失し、斎田も没収されたが、天正17年（1589年）に傳誉が再建し、その後[[天保]]11年（1840年）火災に遭い、[[慶応]]元年（1865年）6月宗浄・歓浄が再建大阪府学務部「大阪府史跡名勝天然記念物」（1974）{{全国書誌番号|73009804}}。 == 文化財 == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|宝篋印塔]] * [[宝篋印塔]]　[[鎌倉時代]] : [[花崗岩]]製、「[[正安]]元年（1299年）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正」と刻銘がある。 * [[梵鐘]] 池田市[[指定文化財]] [[江戸時代]] : [[寛永]]九壬申年（1632年）の銘があり、[[池田市]]内最古のものの一つ。 == 出典 == == 関連項目 == * [[鉢塚古墳 (池田市)]] * [[五社神社 (池田市)]] * [[一乗院 (池田市)]] {{commons|Category:Sakain}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しやかいん}} [[Category:大阪府の寺]] [[Category:高野山真言宗の寺院]] [[Category:池田市の建築物]] [[Category:池田市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国八十八箇所]] {{pref-stub|pref=大阪府}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}}

revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |画像=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |画像説明=山門 |所在地=大阪府池田市鉢塚3丁目4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号 =鉢多羅山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 =[[高野山真言宗]] |本尊 =[[釈迦如来]] |創建年 = （伝）[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]） |開基 =（伝）[[行基]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称 = 尊鉢厄神 |札所等 =[[摂津八十八箇所]]第57番 |文化財 =宝筐印塔（重要美術品）
梵鐘（市指定文化財） |公式HP = http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名 = 尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''釈迦院'''（しゃかいん）は、[[大阪府]][[池田市]]にある[[高野山真言宗]]の寺院。厄除けの寺として知られ、通称は'''尊鉢厄神'''（そんぱちやくじん）と呼ばれる。 == 歴史 == 寺伝によれば、[[神功皇后]]が[[百済]]より[[釈尊]]の[[仏舎利]]多羅宝鉢を得て凱旋し、[[仏教]]がまだ広まっていないので、[[仲哀天皇]]の御代、散逸を恐れて鉢塚に隠した。 その後、[[行基]]が霊夢により石窟より仏舎利を掘出し、[[聖武天皇]]の勅命で精舎を建立。また、行基は自ら[[観音菩薩]]・[[不動明王]]・[[毘沙門天]]像を刻して仏舎利とともに安置。寺を鉢多羅山若王寺釈迦院と号し、村を尊鉢と名づけ、[[斎田]]300石を賜った。 [[天正]]年間（1573年～1592年）の兵火で[[若王寺]]は[[堂宇]]と寺宝を焼失し、斎田も没収されたが、天正17年（1589年）に傳誉が再建し、その後[[天保]]11年（1840年）火災に遭い、[[慶応]]元年（1865年）6月宗浄・歓浄が再建大阪府学務部「大阪府史跡名勝天然記念物」（1974）{{全国書誌番号|73009804}}。 == 文化財 == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|宝篋印塔]] ; 重要美術品 * 宝篋印塔 - 鎌倉時代、[[花崗岩]]製、「[[正安]]元年（1299年）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正」と刻銘がある。1935年、[[重要美術品]]に認定[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]、大阪府サイト（「池田市」をクリック）。 ; 池田市指定文化財 * 梵鐘 - 江戸時代、[[寛永]]九壬申年（1632年）の銘があり、[[池田市]]内最古のものの一つ。 == 出典 == == 外部リンク == *[http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html 摂津国八十八巡礼 第57番 若王寺 釈迦院] *[https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ 尊鉢厄神 釈迦院：JRおでかけネット] {{commons|Category:Sakain}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しやかいん}} [[Category:大阪府の寺]] [[Category:高野山真言宗の寺院]] [[Category:池田市の建築物]] [[Category:池田市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国八十八箇所]] {{Buddhism-stub}}

{{日本の寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |画像=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |画像説明=山門 |所在地=大阪府池田市鉢塚3丁目4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号 =鉢多羅山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 =[[高野山真言宗]] |本尊 =[[釈迦如来]] |創建年 = （伝）[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]） |開基 =（伝）[[行基]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称 = 尊鉢厄神 |札所等 =[[摂津八十八箇所]]第57番 |文化財 =宝筐印塔（重要美術品）

{{RFD notice|'''対象リダイレクト:'''[[Wikipedia:リダイレクトの削除依頼/受付#RFD多羅山若王寺|多羅山若王寺（受付依頼）]]、[[Wikipedia:リダイレクトの削除依頼/受付#RFD若王寺釋迦院|若王寺釋迦院（受付依頼）]]、[[Wikipedia:リダイレクトの削除依頼/受付#RFD鉢多羅山釈迦院|鉢多羅山釈迦院（受付依頼）]]、[[Wikipedia:リダイレクトの削除依頼/受付#RFD鉢多羅山若王寺釈迦院|鉢多羅山若王寺釈迦院（受付依頼）]]、[[Wikipedia:リダイレクトの削除依頼/受付#RFD鉢多羅山若王寺釋迦院|鉢多羅山若王寺釋迦院（受付依頼）]]|※編集上のご注意！このテンプレートはBotによって自動貼付、および審議終了後およそ1週間で自動除去されます。審議途中でリダイレクト先を変更した場合や記事に置き換えた場合、またリダイレクトが削除された後でもテンプレートを取り除かないでください。前後に使用されているnoinclude文も同様です。手動で取り除いた場合、再度自動貼付されることがあります。}} {{日本の寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |画像=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |画像説明=山門 |所在地=大阪府池田市鉢塚3丁目4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号 =鉢多羅山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 =[[高野山真言宗]] |本尊 =[[釈迦如来]] |創建年 = （伝）[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]） |開基 =（伝）[[行基]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称 = 尊鉢厄神 |札所等 =[[摂津八十八箇所]]第57番 |文化財 =宝筐印塔（重要美術品）
梵鐘（市指定文化財） |公式HP = http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名 = 尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''釈迦院'''（しゃかいん）は、[[大阪府]][[池田市]]にある[[高野山真言宗]]の寺院。厄除けの寺として知られ、通称は'''尊鉢厄神'''（そんぱちやくじん）と呼ばれる。 == 歴史 == 寺伝によれば、[[神功皇后]]が[[百済]]より[[釈尊]]の[[仏舎利]]多羅宝鉢を得て凱旋し、[[仏教]]がまだ広まっていないので、[[仲哀天皇]]の御代、散逸を恐れて鉢塚に隠した。その後、[[行基]]が霊夢により石窟より仏舎利を掘出し、[[聖武天皇]]の勅命で精舎を建立。また、行基は自ら[[観音菩薩]]・[[不動明王]]・[[毘沙門天]]像を刻して仏舎利とともに安置。寺を鉢多羅山若王寺釈迦院と号し、村を尊鉢と名づけ、[[斎田]]300石を賜った。[[天正]]年間（1573年 - 1592年）の兵火で若王寺は[[堂宇]]と寺宝を焼失し、斎田も没収されたが、天正17年（1589年）に傳誉が再建し、その後[[天保]]11年（1840年）火災に遭い、[[慶応]]元年（1865年）6月宗浄・歓浄が再建大阪府学務部「大阪府史跡名勝天然記念物」（1974）{{全国書誌番号|73009804}}。 == 文化財 == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|宝篋印塔]] ; 重要美術品 * 宝篋印塔 - 鎌倉時代、[[花崗岩]]製、「[[正安]]元年（1299年）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正」と刻銘がある。1935年、[[重要美術品]]に認定[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]、大阪府サイト（「池田市」をクリック）。 ; 池田市指定文化財 * 梵鐘 - 江戸時代、[[寛永]]九壬申年（1632年）の銘があり、池田市内最古のものの一つ。 == 出典 == == 関連項目 == * [[一乗院 (池田市)]] * [[五社神社 (池田市)|五社神社]]（[[五社大明神]]、五智如来） == 外部リンク == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html 摂津国八十八巡礼 第57番 若王寺 釈迦院] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ 尊鉢厄神 釈迦院：JRおでかけネット] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しやかいん}} [[Category:大阪府の寺]] [[Category:高野山真言宗の寺院]] [[Category:池田市の建築物]] [[Category:池田市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国八十八箇所]]

{{日本の寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |画像=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |画像説明=山門 |所在地=大阪府池田市鉢塚3丁目4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号 =鉢多羅山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 =[[高野山真言宗]] |本尊 =[[釈迦如来]] |創建年 = （伝）[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]） |開基 =（伝）[[行基]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称 = 尊鉢厄神 |札所等 =[[摂津八十八箇所]]第57番 |文化財 =宝筐印塔（重要美術品）
梵鐘（市指定文化財） |公式HP = http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名 = 尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''釈迦院'''（しゃかいん）は、[[大阪府]][[池田市]]にある[[高野山真言宗]]の寺院。厄除けの寺として知られ、通称は'''尊鉢厄神'''（そんぱちやくじん）と呼ばれる。 == 歴史 == 寺伝によれば、[[神功皇后]]が[[百済]]より[[釈尊]]の[[仏舎利]]多羅宝鉢を得て凱旋し、[[仏教]]がまだ広まっていないので、[[仲哀天皇]]の御代、散逸を恐れて鉢塚に隠した。その後、[[行基]]が霊夢により石窟より仏舎利を掘出し、[[聖武天皇]]の勅命で精舎を建立。また、行基は自ら[[観音菩薩]]・[[不動明王]]・[[毘沙門天]]像を刻して仏舎利とともに安置。寺を鉢多羅山若王寺釈迦院と号し、村を尊鉢と名づけ、[[斎田]]300石を賜った。[[天正]]年間（1573年 - 1592年）の兵火で若王寺は[[堂宇]]と寺宝を焼失し、斎田も没収されたが、天正17年（1589年）に傳誉が再建し、その後[[天保]]11年（1840年）火災に遭い、[[慶応]]元年（1865年）6月宗浄・歓浄が再建大阪府学務部「大阪府史跡名勝天然記念物」（1974）{{全国書誌番号|73009804}}。 == 文化財 == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|宝篋印塔]] ; 重要美術品 * 宝篋印塔 - 鎌倉時代、[[花崗岩]]製、「[[正安]]元年（1299年）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正」と刻銘がある。1935年、[[重要美術品]]に認定[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]、大阪府サイト（「池田市」をクリック）。 ; 池田市指定文化財 * 梵鐘 - 江戸時代、[[寛永]]九壬申年（1632年）の銘があり、池田市内最古のものの一つ。 == 出典 == == 関連項目 == * [[一乗院 (池田市)]] * [[五社神社 (池田市)|五社神社]]（[[五社大明神]]、五智如来） == 外部リンク == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html 摂津国八十八巡礼 第57番 若王寺 釈迦院] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ 尊鉢厄神 釈迦院：JRおでかけネット] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しやかいん}} [[Category:大阪府の寺]] [[Category:高野山真言宗の寺院]] [[Category:池田市の建築物]] [[Category:池田市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国八十八箇所]]

釈迦院 (池田市)
[[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]][[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]]

釈迦院 (池田市) [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]][[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] {{寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |画像=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |画像説明=山門 |所在地=Osaka Prefecture, Ikeda City, Hachizuka 3-chome, 4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号=鉢多羅山 |院号= |宗旨= |宗派={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} |本尊={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}} |創建年=(Traditional) {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} 1 (724) |開基=(Traditional) {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} |中興年= |中興= |正式名=鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称=尊鉢厄神 |札所等={{ill|Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|ja|摂津八十八箇所}} No. 57 |文化財=Hōkyōintō (Important Cultural Property)
Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}}, {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299年）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[ja:釈迦院]]

Inscribed with "正安元年（1299年）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[category:most recent 500]][[ja:釈迦院]]

釈迦院 (池田市) [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]][[category:most recent 500]][[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] {{寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |画像=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |画像説明=山門 |所在地=Osaka Prefecture, Ikeda City, Hachizuka 3-chome, 4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号=鉢多羅山 |院号= |宗旨= |宗派={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} |本尊={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}} |創建年=(Traditional) {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} 1 (724) |開基=(Traditional) {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} |中興年= |中興= |正式名=鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称=尊鉢厄神 |札所等={{ill|Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|ja|摂津八十八箇所}} No. 57 |文化財=Hōkyōintō (Important Cultural Property)
Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}}, {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299年）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[category:most recent 500]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:釈迦院]]

釈迦院 (池田市) [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]][[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] {{寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |画像=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |画像説明=山門 |所在地=Osaka Prefecture, Ikeda City, Hachizuka 3-chome, 4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号=鉢多羅山 |院号= |宗旨= |宗派={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} |本尊={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}} |創建年=(Traditional) {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} 1 (724) |開基=(Traditional) {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} |中興年= |中興= |正式名=鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称=尊鉢厄神 |札所等={{ill|Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|ja|摂津八十八箇所}} No. 57 |文化財=Hōkyōintō (Important Cultural Property)
Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}}, {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299年）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]]

Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}}, {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299年）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]]

釈迦院 (池田市) [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]][[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] {{寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |画像=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |画像説明=山門 |所在地=Osaka Prefecture, Ikeda City, Hachizuka 3-chome, 4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号=鉢多羅山 |院号= |宗旨= |宗派={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} |本尊={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}} |創建年=(Traditional) {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} 1 (724) |開基=(Traditional) {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} |中興年= |中興= |正式名=鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称=尊鉢厄神 |札所等={{ill|Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|ja|摂津八十八箇所}} No. 57 |文化財=Hōkyōintō (Important Cultural Property)
Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}}, {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299年）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:New]]

Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}}, {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299年）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:qq 7]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}}, {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299年）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:qq 7]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:qq]]

Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 7]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}}, {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299年）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 7]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:qq]]

Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}}, {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299年）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 7]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

釈迦院 (池田市) [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]][[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] {{寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |image=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |image説明=山門 |所在地=Osaka Prefecture, Ikeda City, Hachizuka 3-chome, 4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号=鉢多羅山 |院号= |宗旨= |宗派={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} |本尊={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来}} |創建年=(Traditional) {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} 1 (724) |開基=(Traditional) {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} |中興年= |中興= |正式名=鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称=尊鉢厄神 |札所等={{ill|Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|ja|摂津八十八箇所}} No. 57 |文化財=Hōkyōintō (Important Cultural Property)
Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} temple located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市}}, Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}}, {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 7]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩}}, {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 7]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]]

One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|12=draft}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 7]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]]

釈迦院 (池田市) {{寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |image=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |image説明=山門 |所在地=Osaka Prefecture, Ikeda City, Hachizuka 3-chome, 4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号=鉢多羅山 |院号= |宗旨= |宗派={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗|12=draft}} |本尊={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|12=draft}} |創建年=(Traditional) {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀|12=draft}} 1 (724) |開基=(Traditional) {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}} |中興年= |中興= |正式名=鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称=尊鉢厄神 |札所等={{ill|Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|ja|摂津八十八箇所|12=draft}} No. 57 |文化財=Hōkyōintō (Important Cultural Property)
Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗|12=draft}} temple located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊|12=draft}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}} unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft}}'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|12=draft}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|12=draft}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|12=draft}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]]

During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|12=draft}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|12=draft}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] }} [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]]

Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗|12=draft}} temple located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊|12=draft}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}} unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft}}'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|12=draft}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|12=draft}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|12=draft}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] }} [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
釈迦院 (池田市) {{寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |image=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |image説明=山門 |所在地=Osaka Prefecture, Ikeda City, Hachizuka 3-chome, 4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号=鉢多羅山 |院号= |宗旨= |宗派={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |本尊={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建年=(Traditional) {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (724) |開基=(Traditional) {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |中興年= |中興= |正式名=鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称=尊鉢厄神 |札所等={{ill|Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|ja|摂津八十八箇所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} No. 57 |文化財=Hōkyōintō (Important Cultural Property)
Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} temple located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}} obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|qq=draft}} unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩|1=Guanyin|2=ja|3=観音菩薩|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] }} [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]]

revision.comment: 26 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:釈迦院 (池田市) up to rev 87555021
revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |画像=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |画像説明=山門 |所在地=大阪府池田市鉢塚3丁目4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号 =鉢多羅山 |院号 = |宗旨 = |宗派 =[[高野山真言宗]] |本尊 =[[釈迦如来]] |創建年 = （伝）[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]） |開基 =（伝）[[行基]] |中興年 = |中興 = |正式名 = 鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称 = 尊鉢厄神 |札所等 =[[摂津八十八箇所]]第57番 |文化財 =宝筐印塔（重要美術品）
梵鐘（市指定文化財） |公式HP = http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名 = 尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''釈迦院'''（しゃかいん）は、[[大阪府]][[池田市]]にある[[高野山真言宗]]の寺院。厄除けの寺として知られ、通称は'''尊鉢厄神'''（そんぱちやくじん）と呼ばれる。 == 歴史 == 寺伝によれば、[[神功皇后]]が[[百済]]より[[釈尊]]の[[仏舎利]]多羅宝鉢を得て凱旋し、[[仏教]]がまだ広まっていないので、[[仲哀天皇]]の御代、散逸を恐れて鉢塚に隠した。その後、[[行基]]が霊夢により石窟より仏舎利を掘出し、[[聖武天皇]]の勅命で精舎を建立。また、行基は自ら[[観音菩薩]]・[[不動明王]]・[[毘沙門天]]像を刻して仏舎利とともに安置。寺を鉢多羅山若王寺釈迦院と号し、村を尊鉢と名づけ、[[斎田]]300石を賜った。[[天正]]年間（1573年 - 1592年）の兵火で若王寺は[[堂宇]]と寺宝を焼失し、斎田も没収されたが、天正17年（1589年）に傳誉が再建し、その後[[天保]]11年（1840年）火災に遭い、[[慶応]]元年（1865年）6月宗浄・歓浄が再建大阪府学務部「大阪府史跡名勝天然記念物」（1974）{{全国書誌番号|73009804}}。 == 文化財 == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|宝篋印塔]] ; 重要美術品 * 宝篋印塔 - 鎌倉時代、[[花崗岩]]製、「[[正安]]元年（1299年）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正」と刻銘がある。1935年、[[重要美術品]]に認定[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]、大阪府サイト（「池田市」をクリック）。 ; 池田市指定文化財 * 梵鐘 - 江戸時代、[[寛永]]九壬申年（1632年）の銘があり、池田市内最古のものの一つ。 == 出典 == == 関連項目 == * [[一乗院 (池田市)]] * [[五社神社 (池田市)|五社神社]]（[[五社大明神]]、五智如来） == 外部リンク == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html 摂津国八十八巡礼 第57番 若王寺 釈迦院] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ 尊鉢厄神 釈迦院：JRおでかけネット] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しやかいん}} [[Category:大阪府の寺]] [[Category:高野山真言宗の寺院]] [[Category:池田市の建築物]] [[Category:池田市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国八十八箇所]] [[Category:聖武天皇]] [[Category:行基|伝]]

梵鐘（市指定文化財） |公式HP = http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名 = 尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''釈迦院'''（しゃかいん）は、[[大阪府]][[池田市]]にある[[高野山真言宗]]の寺院。厄除けの寺として知られ、通称は'''尊鉢厄神'''（そんぱちやくじん）と呼ばれる。 == 歴史 == 寺伝によれば、[[神功皇后]]が[[百済]]より[[釈尊]]の[[仏舎利]]多羅宝鉢を得て凱旋し、[[仏教]]がまだ広まっていないので、[[仲哀天皇]]の御代、散逸を恐れて鉢塚に隠した。その後、[[行基]]が霊夢により石窟より仏舎利を掘出し、[[聖武天皇]]の勅命で精舎を建立。また、行基は自ら[[観音菩薩]]・[[不動明王]]・[[毘沙門天]]像を刻して仏舎利とともに安置。寺を鉢多羅山若王寺釈迦院と号し、村を尊鉢と名づけ、[[斎田]]300石を賜った。[[天正]]年間（1573年 - 1592年）の兵火で若王寺は[[堂宇]]と寺宝を焼失し、斎田も没収されたが、天正17年（1589年）に傳誉が再建し、その後[[天保]]11年（1840年）火災に遭い、[[慶応]]元年（1865年）6月宗浄・歓浄が再建大阪府学務部「大阪府史跡名勝天然記念物」（1974）{{全国書誌番号|73009804}}。 == 文化財 == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|宝篋印塔]] ; 重要美術品 * 宝篋印塔 - 鎌倉時代、[[花崗岩]]製、「[[正安]]元年（1299年）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正」と刻銘がある。1935年、[[重要美術品]]に認定[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]、大阪府サイト（「池田市」をクリック）。 ; 池田市指定文化財 * 梵鐘 - 江戸時代、[[寛永]]九壬申年（1632年）の銘があり、池田市内最古のものの一つ。 == 出典 == == 関連項目 == * [[一乗院 (池田市)]] * [[五社神社 (池田市)|五社神社]]（[[五社大明神]]、五智如来） == 外部リンク == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html 摂津国八十八巡礼 第57番 若王寺 釈迦院] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ 尊鉢厄神 釈迦院：JRおでかけネット] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しやかいん}} [[Category:大阪府の寺]] [[Category:高野山真言宗の寺院]] [[Category:池田市の建築物]] [[Category:池田市の歴史]] [[Category:摂津国八十八箇所]] [[Category:聖武天皇]] [[Category:行基|伝]]

釈迦院 (池田市) {{寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |image=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |image説明=山門 |所在地=Osaka Prefecture, Ikeda City, Hachizuka 3-chome, 4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号=鉢多羅山 |院号= |宗旨= |宗派={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |本尊={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建年=(Traditional) {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (724) |開基=(Traditional) {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |中興年= |中興= |正式名=鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称=尊鉢厄神 |札所等={{ill|Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|ja|摂津八十八箇所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} No. 57 |文化財=Hōkyōintō (Important Cultural Property)
Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} temple located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}} obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|qq=draft}} unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩|1=Guanyin|2=ja|3=観音菩薩|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] }} [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] {{translated page|ja|釈迦院 (池田市)|version=87555021|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦院 (池田市)|version=87555021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] }} [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]]

釈迦院 (池田市) {{寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |image=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |image説明=山門 |所在地=Osaka Prefecture, Ikeda City, Hachizuka 3-chome, 4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号=鉢多羅山 |院号= |宗旨= |宗派={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |本尊={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建年=(Traditional) {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (724) |開基=(Traditional) {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |中興年= |中興= |正式名=鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称=尊鉢厄神 |札所等={{ill|Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|ja|摂津八十八箇所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} No. 57 |文化財=Hōkyōintō (Important Cultural Property)
Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}} temple located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}} obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|qq=draft}} unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩|1=Guanyin|2=ja|3=観音菩薩|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦院 (池田市)|version=87555021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] }} [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]]

One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦院 (池田市)|version=87555021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]]
