revision.text:
釈迦院 (池田市) {{寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |image=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |image説明=山門 |所在地=Osaka Prefecture, Ikeda City, Hachizuka 3-chome, 4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号=鉢多羅山 |院号= |宗旨= |宗派={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qq=}} |本尊={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}} |創建年=(Traditional) {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki|qq=}} 1 (724) |開基=(Traditional) {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōki|qq=}} |中興年= |中興= |正式名=鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称=尊鉢厄神 |札所等={{ill|Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|ja|摂津八十八箇所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|qq=}} No. 57 |文化財=Hōkyōintō (Important Cultural Property)
Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qq=}} temple located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda, Osaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū|qq=}} obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūai|qq=}}'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōki|qq=}} unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōmu|qq=}}'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩|1=Guanyin|2=ja|3=観音菩薩|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon Bosatsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Guanyin|qq=}}, {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myōō|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala|2=ja|3=不動明王|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bishamonten|qq=}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō era|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qq=}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpō|qq=}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō|qq=}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|qq=}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦院 (池田市)|version=87555021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū Temples]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]]

釈迦院 (池田市) {{寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |image=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |image説明=山門 |所在地=Osaka Prefecture, Ikeda City, Hachizuka 3-chome, 4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号=鉢多羅山 |院号= |宗旨= |宗派={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qq=}} |本尊={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}} |創建年=(Traditional) {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinki|qq=}} 1 (724) |開基=(Traditional) {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōki|qq=}} |中興年= |中興= |正式名=鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称=尊鉢厄神 |札所等={{ill|Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|ja|摂津八十八箇所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|qq=}} No. 57 |文化財=Hōkyōintō (Important Cultural Property)
Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qq=}} temple located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|ja|池田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda, Osaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress {{ill|Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū|qq=}} obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor {{ill|Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūai|qq=}}'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōki|qq=}} unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōmu|qq=}}'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩|1=Kannon|lt=Guanyin|2=ja|3=観音菩薩|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon Bosatsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Guanyin|qq=}}, {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myōō|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala|2=ja|3=不動明王|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bishamonten|qq=}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō era|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō era|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qq=}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpō|qq=}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō|qq=}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'ei|qq=}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦院 (池田市)|version=87555021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]]

Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Kannon Bosatsu|ja|観音菩薩|1=Kannon|lt=Guanyin|2=ja|3=観音菩薩|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon Bosatsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Guanyin|qq=}}, {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|1=Acala|lt=Fudō Myōō|2=ja|3=不動明王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudō Myōō|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala|2=ja|3=不動明王|13=User:Immanuelle/Acala|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bishamonten|ja|毘沙門天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bishamonten|qq=}}, enshrining them with the relic. revision.text:
釈迦院 (池田市) {{寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |image=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |image説明=山門 |所在地=Osaka Prefecture, Ikeda City, Hachizuka 3-chome, 4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号=鉢多羅山 |院号= |宗旨= |宗派=[[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] |本尊={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}} |創建年=(Traditional) [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 1 (724) |開基=(Traditional) [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] |中興年= |中興= |正式名=鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称=尊鉢厄神 |札所等={{ill|Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|ja|摂津八十八箇所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|qq=}} No. 57 |文化財=Hōkyōintō (Important Cultural Property)
Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] temple located in [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]] obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]]'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]]'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]], [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦院 (池田市)|version=87555021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]]

revision.text:
釈迦院 (池田市) {{寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |image=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |image説明=山門 |所在地=Osaka Prefecture, Ikeda City, Hachizuka 3-chome, 4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号=鉢多羅山 |院号= |宗旨= |宗派=[[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] |本尊={{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈迦如来|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}} |創建年=(Traditional) [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 1 (724) |開基=(Traditional) [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] |中興年= |中興= |正式名=鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称=尊鉢厄神 |札所等={{ill|Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|ja|摂津八十八箇所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|qq=}} No. 57 |文化財=Hōkyōintō (Important Cultural Property)
Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] temple located in [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]] obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]]'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]]'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]], [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦院 (池田市)|version=87555021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:摂津国八十八箇所]]

Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦院 (池田市)|version=87555021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:摂津国八十八箇所]] [[Category:Buildings in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:池田市の歴史]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]]

Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] temple located in [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]] obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]]'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]]'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]], [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632). Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] temple located in [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]] obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]]'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]]'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]], [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦院 (池田市)|version=87555021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Settsukoku Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Buildings in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]]

Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]] obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni|ja|釈尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]]'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]]'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]], [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦院 (池田市)|version=87555021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] }} [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Settsukoku Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Buildings in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]]

Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] temple located in [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]] obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]]'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]]'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]], [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦院 (池田市)|version=87555021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] }} [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Settsukoku Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Buildings in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|qq=|1=Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352380}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦院 (池田市)|version=87555021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] }} [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Settsukoku Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Buildings in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Sakain01.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qid=Q3077357}}|prefecture={{ill|Ikeda|qid=Q817965}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.817889|N|135.43944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11644541]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} 釈迦院 (池田市) {{寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |image=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |image説明=山門 |所在地=Osaka Prefecture, Ikeda City, Hachizuka 3-chome, 4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号=鉢多羅山 |院号= |宗旨= |宗派=[[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] |本尊={{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈迦如来|1=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}} |創建年=(Traditional) [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 1 (724) |開基=(Traditional) [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] |中興年= |中興= |正式名=鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称=尊鉢厄神 |札所等={{ill|Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|ja|摂津八十八箇所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂津国八十八箇所|1=Settsukoku Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11078809}} No. 57 |文化財=Hōkyōintō (Important Cultural Property)
Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] temple located in [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]] obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]]'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]]'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]], [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|qq=|1=Ichijoin Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352380}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦院 (池田市)|version=87555021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] }} [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Settsukoku Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Buildings in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]][[Category:qq]]

One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|qq=|1=Ichijoin Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352380}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦院 (池田市)|version=87555021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] }} [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Settsukoku Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Buildings in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]]

{{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Sakain01.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qid=Q3077357}}|prefecture={{ill|Ikeda|qid=Q817965}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.817889|N|135.43944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11644541]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} 釈迦院 (池田市) {{寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |image=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |image説明=山門 |所在地=Osaka Prefecture, Ikeda City, Hachizuka 3-chome, 4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号=鉢多羅山 |院号= |宗旨= |宗派=[[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] |本尊={{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈迦如来|1=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}} |創建年=(Traditional) [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 1 (724) |開基=(Traditional) [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] |中興年= |中興= |正式名=鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称=尊鉢厄神 |札所等={{ill|Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|ja|摂津八十八箇所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂津国八十八箇所|1=Settsukoku Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11078809}} No. 57 |文化財=Hōkyōintō (Important Cultural Property)
Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] temple located in [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]] obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]]'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]]'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]], [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|qq=|1=Ichijoin Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352380}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦院 (池田市)|version=87555021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] }} [[sn:Shakain (Ikeda)]] [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Settsukoku Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Buildings in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Shakain (Ikeda)]] {{wikidata link|Q11644541}}

One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|qq=|1=Ichijoin Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352380}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦院 (池田市)|version=87555021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] }} [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Settsukoku Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Buildings in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11644541}}

{{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Sakain01.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qid=Q3077357}}|prefecture={{ill|Ikeda|qid=Q817965}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.817889|N|135.43944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11644541]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} 釈迦院 (池田市) {{寺院 |名称=釈迦院 |image=[[File:Sakain01.jpg|250px|]] |image説明=山門 |所在地=Osaka Prefecture, Ikeda City, Hachizuka 3-chome, 4-6 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|4.4|N|135|26|22|E|region:JP}} |山号=鉢多羅山 |院号= |宗旨= |宗派=[[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] |本尊={{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈迦如来|1=Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=}} |創建年=(Traditional) [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 1 (724) |開基=(Traditional) [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] |中興年= |中興= |正式名=鉢多羅山釈迦院 |別称=尊鉢厄神 |札所等={{ill|Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|ja|摂津八十八箇所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Eighty-Eight Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂津国八十八箇所|1=Settsukoku Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11078809}} No. 57 |文化財=Hōkyōintō (Important Cultural Property)
Bonshō (City Designated Cultural Property) |公式HP=http://www.sonpachi.com/ |公式HP名=尊鉢厄神 釈迦院 }} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] temple located in [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]] obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]]'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]]'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]], [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}} == Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City"). ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|qq=|1=Ichijoin Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352380}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas) == External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net] {{Commonscat|Sakain}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|釈迦院 (池田市)|version=87555021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Emperor Shōmu]] [[Category:Gyōki]] }} [[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] [[ja:釈迦院]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Settsukoku Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Buildings in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] {{wikidata link|Q11644541}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Sakain01.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qid=Q3077357}}|prefecture={{ill|Ikeda|qid=Q817965}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.817889|N|135.43944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] temple located in [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神).
== History == According to temple tradition, Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]] obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]]'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]]'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]], [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.<ref>Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City").</ref> ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City.

Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Sakain01.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|WD=Q3077357}}|prefecture={{ill|Ikeda|WD=Q817965}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.817889|N|135.43944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] temple located in [[:en:Ikeda, Osaka|Ikeda, Osaka]], Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神).
== History == According to temple tradition, Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]] obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]]'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]]'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]], [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.<ref>Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City").</ref> ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632). == History == According to temple tradition, Empress [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū]] obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Chūai]]'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]]'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]], [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]], and [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]], enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.<ref>Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City").</ref> ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City.

Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City").</ref> ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City.
== References == <references/> == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|1=Ichijoin Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352380}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas)

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:釈迦院 (池田市)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Sakain01.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|WD=Q3077357}}|prefecture={{ill|Ikeda|WD=Q817965}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.817889|N|135.43944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Shakain''' (釈迦院) is a {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|WD=Q3077357|lt=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}} temple located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|en|Ikeda, Osaka|WD=Q817965|lt=Ikeda, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture. Known as a temple for warding off misfortune, it is commonly referred to as '''Sonpachi Yakujin''' (尊鉢厄神).
== History == According to temple tradition, Empress {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū}} obtained a relic of {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja|釈尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 釈迦}} called Tara Hōbatsu from Baekje upon her triumphant return. During Emperor {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Chūai}}'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Shōmu}}'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva}}, {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|en|Vaiśravaṇa|WD=Q866315|lt=Bishamonten}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.<ref>Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City").</ref> ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City.
== References == <references/> == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|1=Ichijoin Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352380}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas)
== External Links == * [http://shj.main.jp/temple57.html Settsu Eighty-Eight Pilgrimage No. 57: Jakōji Shakain] * [https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/1342/ Sonpachi Yakujin Shakain: JR Odekake Net]

During Emperor {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}'s reign, fearing the relic might be lost due to Buddhism not being widespread, it was hidden in Hachizuka. Later, {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki|1=Gyōki}} unearthed the relic from a cave following a divine vision and, under Emperor {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}}'s order, established a temple. Gyōki also carved images of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva|1=Avalokiteśvara}}, {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}}, and {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|en|Vaiśravaṇa|WD=Q866315|lt=Bishamonten|1=Vaiśravaṇa}}, enshrining them with the relic. The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.<ref>Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties == [[File:Sｙakain03.jpg|thumb|left|Hōkyōintō]] ; Important Cultural Property * Hōkyōintō - A Kamakura period pagoda made of granite. Inscribed with "正安元年（1299th year ）十二月廿五日 願主藤原景正" (December 25, 1299, Prayer Offerer Fujiwara no Kagemasa). Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City").</ref> ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}} 9 (1632). One of the oldest in Ikeda City.
== References == <references/> == See Also == * {{ill|Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|ja|一乗院 (池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichijō-in (Ikeda)|1=Ichijo-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352380}} * {{ill|Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|ja|五社神社 (池田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha Shrine (Ikeda)|qq=}} (also known as Gosha Daimyōjin or Five Wisdom Buddhas)

The temple was named "Hachitarasan Jakōji Shakain," and the village was renamed "Sonpachi," receiving 300 koku of sacred rice fields. During the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1573–1592), the temple was destroyed by fire and its rice fields confiscated. It was rebuilt in Tenshō 17 (1589) by Den'yo, but a subsequent fire in {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 11 (1840) led to another reconstruction in {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} 1 (1865) by Sōjō and Kanjō.<ref>Osaka Prefecture Education Department, "Osaka Prefecture Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments" (1974) {{全国書誌番号|73009804}}</ref>

Recognized as an Important Cultural Property in 1935.<ref>[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties List] (click on "Ikeda City").</ref> ; Ikeda City Designated Cultural Property * Bonshō - An Edo period bell inscribed with the year {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}} 9 (1632).
